memory.summary: The quiz is locked in for Thursday 09:00, and we’re still meeting at 19:00 today—so time is tight. Also, Assignment A is still only half done, which raises my stress because I can’t catch up comfortably.
I still don’t have enough time to catch up comfortably: the quiz is locked for Thursday 09:00 and Assignment A is only half done. With my low energy and big sleep debt, my focus is already slipping.
Teammate_A just asked if the meeting materials are ready, which I haven’t fully confirmed yet. With my sleep-debt and low energy, I’m more anxious than I want to be about slipping deadlines.
Nothing new landed, but I’m even more depleted now—sleep debt is still heavy and my stress is high. It confirms I’m operating on razor-thin focus, so any small delay will feel irreversible.

I hit my fatigue threshold tonight—energy is basically gone—so the situation feels less like a manageable delay and more like an approaching point of no return. I’m still stressed, but now I can’t rely on focus to fix it.

desires: Don’t look unreliable to teammate_A / don’t be the reason the meeting materials aren’t ready.
Avoid the “point of no return” feeling by getting at least one hard deadline (quiz or Assignment A) to a safe, locked-in status.
Protect my GPA/achievement profile—so the quiz prep has to happen even if I’m running on fumes.
Not have to face calling out how far behind I am—so I want the problem to feel solvable without admitting it.

goals.description: Assignment A: complete the remaining ~11% core deliverable, run/verify it works once end-to-end, then stop at “locked & correct” (no polishing).
goals.rationale: This is the hard/irreversible fear item. Even with energy tanking, I need one safe, locked status before sleep-debt turns everything into panic.

goals.description: Quiz prep (45–60 min): do highest-yield review → short practice set → write a misses checklist limited to 3–5 fixes only.
goals.rationale: Protect my GPA profile. I can’t afford freezing, so I’m forcing a focused block even if I feel unreliable.

goals.description: Teammate update (async): after any quick proof of progress (even a small step), send a short confirmation that materials are ready + promise immediate final share after Assignment A is fully locked.
goals.rationale: Avoidant conflict-minimizing move. It reduces the risk of being “the reason” things aren’t ready without me having to confess more than necessary.

goals.description: Team meeting: draft a 30-second update (today’s done + next concrete step) and rehearse once out loud.
goals.rationale: Low drama effort. I can show progress without overexplaining or narrating how behind I feel.

goals.description: Office-hours readiness check: if I get stuck during Assignment A or quiz prep, draft exactly 1 question (error + expected behavior) and message the professor to confirm a help slot.
goals.rationale: I hate admitting failure, but a single decisive question prevents stress from hardening into an avoidant spiral.

agent.last_reasoning: Goal g1: push Assignment A the remaining ~11% to “locked & correct,” since that’s the irreversible fear item. I’m low-energy (sleep-debt 6, energy 0), but starting now buys enough end-to-end verification to avoid panic later; side effect is energy drop, but I’ll stop once it’s correct.

goals.description: Finish Assignment A to a “safe next step” state: complete the remaining half so I can stop thinking about it tonight (aim: finish core deliverable, not perfection).
goals.rationale: It’s hard and already 0.5 left—this is the irreversible-fear item. If I don’t lock it down now, tomorrow turns into panic.

goals.description: Start quiz_prep immediately: do one focused prep block (e.g., review topics + complete a practice set) for the Thu 09:00 quiz.
goals.rationale: The quiz is hard and un-started; sleep-debt + performance penalty is the threat. I’m avoiding drama by making progress early.

goals.description: At the 19:00 team meeting, show effort: be ready with a 30-second status update (what I completed for the project today + what I’m doing next).
goals.rationale: Meeting is soft but socially costly if I look flaky. Even overloaded, I can show movement without opening emotional vulnerability.

goals.description: Do a quick self-check after meeting/around late evening: decide whether I actually need professor help for Assignment A, but only if I’m blocked (otherwise keep scrambling).
goals.rationale: I hate the vibe of admitting I’m struggling, so I’m deferring help-seeking unless there’s a genuine hard block.

desires: Finish Assignment A tonight enough that it doesn’t feel like I’m falling behind in a way that’s *irreversible* tomorrow.
Stay ahead of the Thursday 9AM quiz so I don’t get hit with that panic where sleep-debt turns into a straight-up performance penalty.
Don’t look flaky at the 19:00 team meeting—at least show effort/progress even if I’m overloaded.
Avoid asking the professor for help because I hate the vibe of admitting I’m struggling; I’d rather scramble than risk sounding unreliable.

desires: Finish Assignment A in a way that makes me look competent—because I can’t afford to feel “behind” to myself or anyone else, especially with how close the deadline is.
Lock in enough quiz prep for Thu 9AM without burning out—so I don’t wake up tomorrow realizing I threw away the only early win.
Avoid a messy, high-drama interaction at the 19:00 team meeting—so I can keep it minimal and not risk being seen as unreliable.
Get one short burst of control (even if the rest is doomed)—to reduce the stress spike in my head right now, since my energy is already tanking.
